source_pr.title: planner: a quick fix to solve the column alias in ORDER BY's subquery
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #42732

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

The column alias is not seeable in ORDER BY's subquery clause.

This pr provides a quick way to fix the issue.

It's not the final solution to the AST resolution problems.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: No concrete flaky failure signature recorded in the case data; PR fixes a planner correctness issue around column alias in ORDER BY subqueries.
failure_signature: No concrete flaky signature recorded; planner correctness fix around ORDER BY subquery alias resolution.
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR fixes a planner correctness issue (column alias resolution in ORDER BY subqueries) and adjusts test/BUILD settings. The case data does not include a concrete flaky signature or a targeted stabilization change for nondeterminism, so keep it as insufficient evidence rather than asserting a specific flaky mechanism.
fix_pattern: Planner correctness fix for alias resolution in ORDER BY subqueries; no clearly evidenced anti-flake stabilization in the case data.

evidence: evidence: race = "on", | tk.MustQuery("SELECT one.a, one.b as b2 FROM t1 one ORDER BY (SELECT two.b FROM t2 two WHERE two.a = one.b)").Check(testkit.Rows("1 1")) (planner/core/issuetest/planner_issue_test.go)
